creator: markus@stoffdv.at
text:
(In reply to Dave Eckhardt from comment #14)

I highly appreciate and second that opinion. And I also don't think this is a lot less dire here.

In fact, 13.2-RELEASE provides a good argumentative underpinning as to why this is a really good idea. This release has been signed by a 1024 bit DSA (which is considered unsafe) key using SHA-1 (which is known to be broken).

https://www.freebsd.org/releases/13.2R/signatures/

Back than I have created a security incident, which you can't view, because - security. Here is the link anyway: https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=274886

The bottom line was: "I don't think it's worth re-signing the 13.2 release hashes"

In my opinion, if releases are signed, there should be a solid process in place, along with easy-to-follow and readily accessible documentation on how to validate these signatures.
